lines: +            Opt a private/loopback/link-local delivery target out of the SSRF
+            guard. Admin role only; a non-admin operator setting this gets 403.
description: Write-only HMAC secret. Omit to keep, "" to clear, value to set.
`""` to clear it, or a new value to replace it. A non-empty secret requires
the master key to be configured.
- `url` is SSRF-validated like the config webhook; `allow_private: true` opts a
+  private/loopback target in. Setting `allow_private` requires the admin role —
+  a non-admin operator sending it gets `403`.
+		writeError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "allow_private requires the admin role")
if err := config.ValidateWebhookURL("url", req.URL, req.AllowPrivate); err != nil {
+		writeError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "allow_private requires the admin role")
if err := config.ValidateWebhookURL("url", req.URL, req.AllowPrivate); err != nil {
+// (GHSA-7rx3-5wx3-5v76): a non-admin operator must not be able to set
+// allow_private, which switches the dispatcher to the unguarded HTTP client and
+	// Create with allow_private → 403, same as every other admin-gated toggle.
+		t.Fatalf("non-admin create with allow_private: %d / %s, want 403", rec.Code, rec.Body.String())
+	// Control: same non-admin key, a public URL without allow_private still works.
+		t.Fatalf("non-admin create (public url): %d / %s, want 201", crec.Code, crec.Body.String())
+	// Update the owned sub to flip allow_private on → 403.
+		t.Fatalf("non-admin update with allow_private: %d / %s, want 403", urec.Code, urec.Body.String())
